name: Ukrainian People's Republic (1917, yellow-blue)

colors.symbolism: the upper stripe — the golden domes (cupolas) of Christian churches; the historic Galician gold lion
the lower stripe — the Dnipro river and the heraldic azure of Galicia

description: A horizontal bicolour — yellow over blue — used as an early, non-official variant of the Ukrainian national flag in 1917 during the founding of the Ukrainian People's Republic.
history: Both blue-yellow and yellow-blue arrangements circulated during the Ukrainian struggle for independence in 1917, with the yellow-on-top version particularly favoured by some émigré circles. The Ukrainian People's Republic ultimately adopted the blue-over-yellow order officially on 22 March 1918, but the yellow-blue variant continued in unofficial use through the UPR period (1917–1921) and persists today in Ukrainian heraldic and diaspora contexts.

features.symbolism: the inverted (yellow-on-top) arrangement that competed with the eventual blue-on-top design during the Ukrainian independence movement
